title: What does Rei know
wikitext:
[]: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Sun Feb 01, 2026 2:04 am Maybe I touched on this elsewhere but I am really interested in the question of what Rei knows--about herself, about NERV, about angels, and everything. I have seen other posters claiming or inferring things they think she knows but I really wonder where they get it--as in clear evidence from the episodes themselves. So, to get the discussion underway here are specific things I am asking about. You can mention other things, and I do not assume everyone agrees about everything. I just want to know what evidence you have as the basis for the claim. Does she know she is a clone? Does she remember most or all of her previous past when her soul enters a new body? How much does she know about the Human Instrumentality Project? Does she know about SEELE? If so what does she know about it? If she knows she was cloned does she know who the original source of the DNA was? Also, did Gendo clone Yui with, or without her knowledge? Shinji was four when Yui died and suppressed the memory. Rei is his same age approximately, so she was born a few years before Yui died. If you disagree, what is your argument? She brought the Lance down to Terminal Dogma and gets her LCL treatments there. But what does she know about Lilith? Interested in various opinions on such matters.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Feb 01, 2026 4:10 pm Rei seems to have no memories of being Lilith, and be unaware of her true nature until Kaworu tips her off. She definitely knows she's a clone because she alludes to it all the time. She's definitely aware of her role in Gendo's plans and thus has enough of that info to understand that SEELE want to control Third Impact, since Gendo is trying to outrun their own timetable, and he's made efforts to make sure she's never spoken to by them and in case of emergency he's probably coached her on what to do about it. She almost definitely knows who she's cloned from, and it's generally the agreed upon interpretation that the first Rei was an accidental product of trying to retrieve Yui from inside Unit-01 (when it was still connected to Lilith). Rei was definitely not born before Yui's death, because she only appeared afterwards, all of the sudden, with no records of her existence before that point; she was artificially aged up if not born at that age. The proof is that the Spare Reis in the Dummy Tank are all the same age as the current Rei, unless you expect to believe that every single Rei clone was batched the exact same day as the first one. Rei definitely knows Lilith exists but doesn't know her full significance until Kaworu gives her more puzzle pieces to work with.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Sun Feb 01, 2026 10:08 pm All the clones are physically the same age, so I assumed they had all been growing up with her. It never occurred to me that she could be "born" as a fully-formed toddler who can already talk. People have to learn to talk, and the body must develop. But I am just thinking about this and not arguing for a theory yet.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Feb 02, 2026 2:53 pm Rei explicitly has her memories backed up and copied into new clones, so it's not unfeasible that they can just download starting information into her, like language sets.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Tue Feb 03, 2026 9:26 pm I thought Ritsuko said her soul itself transfers. She also said the dummy plug couldn't be a perfect copy of a soul due to inherent technical limitations. In NGE we see souls retaining their memories (specifically Yui) outside the body so it seems like the memories would transfer along with her soul.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 04, 2026 3:21 pm Well, yes, they have to transfer the soul, but they're digitally copying and transferring her memories. The Dummy Plug system is entirely built on this, and their having to do so is why, for example, Rei III doesn't remember Rei II's final moments. That they have to manually transcribe Rei's memories and identities into her might have to do with Lilith being her true identity, or Gendo not wanting to leave things up to chance and/or wanting to manually control her self-identity.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Mon Feb 09, 2026 8:41 pm Here is the thing, Shinji loses his body but his soul gets a new or restored body, and he remembers it all. So the soul as understood in evangelion does retain memories, and can be fully cognizant in between bodies (as seen during instrumentality) so there is no separate need for memories to copied, as I see it.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 11, 2026 3:01 pm Right, but Shinji's reconstructing his own body. Rei's body is not her's. She's Lilith. And she's at best a fragment of the full soul. NERV also does not have perfect scientific control over the soul, regardless, and they have incentive to control Rei's memories aside.

[]: Re: What does Rei know?             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Sun Feb 15, 2026 11:17 pm There might be more than one theory that preserves the information we get in the show. But in any case it's interesting to talk about.
